revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Stundengottheiten]] {{See Wiktionary|Altägyptische Stundengottheiten}}
'''Hour deities''' in {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} are associated with the hours of the night in the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}} and {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}. Initially unnamed as a group, they are referenced in texts such as the {{ill|Book of Gates|en|Book of Gates|qid=Q32137|lt=Book of Gates|1=Book of Gates}}. These deities were connected to the {{ill|Acronychal rising|de|akronychische Aufgänge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychal rising|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} and nightly {{ill|Culmination (astronomy)|de|Kulminationen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Culmination (astronomy)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} of the {{ill|Decans|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decans|qid=Q804527}}. Additionally, they served as protectors of the deceased.
== Background == === Origins === The origins of the hour deities in Egyptian history are uncertain. Their connection to the {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qid=Q142353|1=Star clock}} representations on Middle Kingdom coffins is well established.
Texts such as the "{{ill|Book of Day|de|Buch vom Tag|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Day|qid=Q997784|1=Book of the Day}}" and "{{ill|Book of Night|de|Buch von der Nacht|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Night|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}" place significant emphasis on the hours. These hours are depicted with titles linked to {{ill|Mythology|de|Mythologie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mythology|qid=Q9134|1=Mythology}} narratives, often related to the "nature of light" or the roles of "guardians of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}} and {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}." Early depictions of daytime hours from the "Book of Day" are found in the temple of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|qid=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}} and were later preserved by the {{ill|Ptolemaic dynasty|en|Ptolemaic dynasty|qid=Q131976|lt=Ptolemaic dynasty|1=Ptolemaic dynasty}}. Nighttime hours from the "Book of Night" appear in early texts such as the "{{ill|List of Gates|de|Liste der Tore|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Gates|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}" ({{ill|Sebechet|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sebechet|qq=}}), where gate names correspond to hour names.
=== Hour Deities in the Amduat === Named depictions of hour goddesses first appear during the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} in the {{ill|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q146055|lt=Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt}} (1550–1292 BCE). In the "{{ill|Amduat|en|Amduat|qid=Q460509|lt=Amduat|1=Amduat}}," hour goddesses function as "guardians of the twelve regions of the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}," which the sun god {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}} traverses during the night. Each station is guarded by an hour goddess, collectively pulling the {{ill|Solar barque|de|Barke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar barque|qid=Q1092993|1=barque}} of Ra.
{| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Egyptology TblHighlight}} |colspan="4" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Hour Goddesses of the Night in the Amduat''' |- {{Egyptology TblHighlight}} !Hour !Name !Hour Gate !Hour Cavern |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |de|First Hour of the Night (Ancient Egypt)|1st Hour]] |''She Who Smashes the Foreheads of Her Enemies'' |''Mistress of Trembling, with High Walls, Leader, Mistress of Destruction'' | |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |2nd Hour |''The Wise One Who Protects Her Lord'' |''{{ill|All-Devourer|de|Allesverschlinger|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/All-Devourer|qid=Q2648719|1=All-Devourer}}'' | |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |3rd Hour |''She Who Splits the {{ill|Ba (Egyptian mythology)|de|Ba (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ba (Egyptian mythology)|qid=Q1129404|1=Ba (Egyptian soul)}}'' |''Robber'' |''Field of the Shore Dwellers'' |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |4th Hour |''The Great One Who Is in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}'' |''The Hidden Drawer'' |''With Living Manifestations'' |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |5th Hour |''The Guide Who Is in Her {{ill|Solar barque|de|Barke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Solar barque|qid=Q1092993|1=barque}}'' |''Gods’ Halt'' |''West'' |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |6th Hour |''Arrival, She Who Shows the Right Path'' |''With Sharp Knives'' |''Waterhole of the Netherworld'' |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |7th Hour |''She Who Repels the Hiu and Beheads the Terrible-Faced One'' |''Gate of {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}'' |''Mysterious Cavern (of Osiris)'' |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |8th Hour |''Mistress of {{ill|Midnight|en|Midnight|qid=Q36402|lt=Midnight|1=Midnight}}'' |''That Stands Without Growing Weary'' |''{{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} of Their Gods'' |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |9th Hour |''The Worshiper Who Protects Her Lord'' |''That Guards the {{ill|Flooding of the Nile|en|Flooding of the Nile|qid=Q1519552|lt=Flooding of the Nile|1=Flooding of the Nile}}'' |''With Protruding Forms'' |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |10th Hour |''The Furious One Who Slaughters the Treacherous'' |''With Great Manifestations, Birthing Forms'' |''With Deep Waters and High Banks'' |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" |11th Hour |''The {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}}ry One, Mistress of the Barque, Who Repels the Adversary at His Rising'' |''Resting Place of the Netherworldly'' |''Edge of the Cavern'' |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" |de|Twelfth Hour of the Night (Ancient Egypt)|12th Hour]] |''She Who Beholds the Perfection of Her Lord'' |''That Which Elevates the Gods'' |''End of {{ill|Keku-semau|de|Keku-semau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keku-semau|qid=Q1738122|1=Q1738122}}'' |}
=== Ptolemaic Continuation === During the {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}} cult, priests depicted hour deities as {{ill|Ritual|de|Ritus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritual|qid=Q628455|1=rite}} guardians, mourning and protecting Osiris’ body. Temple inscriptions from {{ill|Dendera|en|Dendera|qid=Q29019|lt=Dendera|1=Dendera}}, {{ill|Edfu|en|Edfu|qid=Q239107|lt=Edfu|1=Edfu}}, and {{ill|Temple of Philae|de|Philae|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Philae|qid=Q1234711|1=Philæ}} document these rituals. Their depiction is tied to {{ill|Egyptian astronomy|en|Egyptian astronomy|qid=Q751843|lt=Ancient Egyptian astronomy|1=Egyptian astronomy}} and the role of {{ill|Stundenwächter|hour watchers]] among Egyptian priests.
== References == * {{ill|Hans Bonnet (Egyptologist)|de|Hans Bonnet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stundenwächter|qid=Q1578787|1=Hans Bonnet}}: ''Lexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte (formerly: Reallexikon der ägyptischen Religionsgeschichte)'', Nikol, Hamburg 2005, ISBN 3-937872-08-6. * {{ill|Wolfgang Helck]], {{ill|Eberhard Otto (Egyptologist)|de|Eberhard Otto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wolfgang Helck]], {{ill|qq=}}: ''Kleines Lexikon der Ägyptologie'', Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1999, ISBN 3-447-04027-0. * Wolfgang Helck, Eberhard Otto: ''Lexikon der Ägyptologie, Bd. 6'', Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1986, ISBN 3-447-02663-4. * {{ill|Erik Hornung|en|Erik Hornung|qid=Q117212|lt=Erik Hornung|1=Erik Hornung}}: ''Die Unterweltsbücher der Ägypter'', Artemis, Düsseldorf 1997, ISBN 3-7608-1061-6. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qid=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5.
{{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Astronomie}}
{{translated page|de|Stundengottheiten|version=195811915|comment=Imported full de history}}

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5.
